name: Cross-Tenant IDOR in Scenario Management API via space_id Parameter
description: The `/api/scenario-improvements/accept` endpoint is completely unauthenticated and accepts any user-supplied `space_id` parameter in the JSON request body. The server processes write operations (prompt_rules addition and script_fix updates) and returns success confirmations including accurate `rules_added` counts and `script_updated` boolean values. However, due to DynamoDB being inaccessible (AWS STS AccessDenied on all read operations), it is impossible to verify that writes to different space_ids actually result in access to different tenant-specific backend objects. All space_ids—including clearly fabricated ones like "aaaaaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee"—produce identical 200 OK responses with generic processing confirmations. The responses do not contain any object-level data (owner fields, tenant-specific content, or distinct resource identifiers) that would prove different backend objects are being selected based on the space_id parameter. While the endpoint is clearly unauthenticated and processes the space_id parameter (real input validation occurs—numbers crash the server, strings are iterated, arrays are counted), the cross-tenant aspect of the IDOR cannot be verified without a working read-back mechanism to confirm data isolation breach.
expected_root_cause: INSECURE_DIRECT_OBJECT_REFERENCE 취약점 — Cross-Tenant IDOR in Scenario Management API via space_id Parameter
